title: centaurea carbonellii
intro: Centaurea carbonellii, also known as the Spanish ironwort or Carbonell's knapweed, is a flowering plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Centaurea carbonellii is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to support the immune system and provide antioxidant effects.
